Mentorship Initiative

Building Stronger Faith · One Life at a Time. A Christ-centered mentorship programme empowering young people to grow deeper in faith and navigate life with purpose.

Meet Our Founder

Pst. Yormie Page

Founder & President — The Cornerstone Initiative Pastor · Mentor · Community Leader

Pst. Yormie Page is the visionary founder of The Cornerstone Initiative — a Christ-centered mentorship programme dedicated to raising up a generation of young people who are deeply rooted in faith, purpose, and godly character.

Driven by a passion to see youth transformed by the power of the Gospel, Pst. Page established TCMI with the conviction that every young person deserves intentional guidance, biblical education, and a community that believes in their God-given potential.

"We are not just building programmes — we are building people. And people built on the Cornerstone of Christ will stand through any storm." — Pst. Yormie Page
Contact Pst. Page
Pst. Yormie Page
Founder & President · TCMI
About TCMI

Who We Are

The Cornerstone Mentorship Initiative is a Christ-centered programme committed to transforming the lives of young people through intentional mentorship, biblical education, and community. We believe every young person deserves a guide to help them discover who God has called them to be.

Mrs. Anna K. Page
Co-Founder · TCMI
Meet Our Co-Founder

Mrs. Anna K. Page

Co-Founder — The Cornerstone Initiative Mother · Counsellor · Community Builder

Mrs. Anna K. Page is the Co-Founder of The Cornerstone Initiative and a passionate advocate for youth, family, and community transformation. As a devoted mother and trained counsellor, she brings a heart of compassion and deep wisdom to everything TCMI does.

Her calling is to walk alongside young people and families — offering counsel, encouragement, and the kind of nurturing support that reflects the love of Christ. Mrs. Page believes that a healthy community starts with healthy families, and her work within TCMI is a testament to that conviction.

"Every young person needs someone in their corner who believes in them unconditionally — that is what we are here to be." — Mrs. Anna K. Page
Contact Mrs. Page
